beats.title: Kalima Slips Into Night
beats.boundaryReason: The session opens with the party bedding down in Nura and Khaled's hut, then pivots into a tense watch scene when Jrain notices Kalima slipping out with a guide while soldiers search the village, before the group decides not to intervene and lets the rest of the night pass into a completed long rest.
dateEvidence: the DM says it is 'the end of the day' rather than full night when the party goes to sleep
the transcript explicitly says 'the rest of the night passes uneventfully' and then grants the long rest, so this beat crosses into the next morning

beats.title: The Baby Speaks Back
beats.boundaryReason: After waking, the party questions the family, hears the baby's unnerving memories of Zahara and a prior fall into the labyrinth, learns Melusa's people believe some children are born with remembering souls, and gathers enough lore about the demiplane, Malquin, and Zahara's circle to choose the troublemakers as their next stop.
dateEvidence: this beat starts immediately after the long rest is completed
the family offers food and asks where the party will go as they wake up, marking a new morning scene
nothing in the conversation suggests another sleep, sunset, or day transition before they leave for Zahara's side of the village

beats.title: Road to the Troublemakers
beats.boundaryReason: The party crosses the village to Iskander, sees the marked doors and quiet aftermath of the soldiers' sweep, and then spends the whole visit extracting the rebels' version of Melusa's history, the crack escape attempts, Malquin's habits, Zahara's hideout, and the rebel movement's actual size and limits.
dateEvidence: the group leaves Nura and Khaled's hut directly after the morning revelations with no intervening rest
the village is active with fishing and daylight labor rather than a new sleep cycle
Iskander's briefing is a continuous daytime conversation that never advances the date beyond the same morning-to-daytime stretch

beats.title: A Plan for Malquin
beats.boundaryReason: Once Iskander's factual briefing runs out, the scene shifts into party strategy: they weigh saving villagers against fixing the wider planar crisis, send a warning to Zahara, stage a conspicuous march toward the palace, and test the strange metal crack before publicly demanding that the bridge be extended.
dateEvidence: this beat follows seamlessly from the Iskander conversation without a rest or scene reset
the group is still operating in the same daylight cycle while moving from private planning into a public demonstration at the bridge site
there is no transcript evidence of a sunset, sleep, or next-day transition before the bridge finally appears

beats.title: Bargain in the Ice Palace
beats.boundaryReason: The party crosses the conjured bridge, enters Malquin's palace, and survives a long unstable negotiation in which the lord veers between regal host, terrified captive, and predatory threat before finally agreeing to let them pass and help later if they can prove the outside world is real and safe.
dateEvidence: the palace audience begins immediately once the bridge extends from the prior beat
the entire exchange with Malquin is one uninterrupted parley with no rest or daybreak cue
nothing in the palace scene suggests the date has changed before the group returns to the guarded crack

beats.title: Back to the Real Chasm
beats.boundaryReason: With Malquin's leave, the party confirms the ice wolf no longer bars them, pushes through the safe crack into the true chasm, discovers the floating stone mechanism and Eidrikas holding it together in agony, learns that time outside has advanced by four extra days, and ends by choosing to hunt the orcs who stole the missing piece next session.
dateEvidence: the beat starts immediately after the palace bargain, so it initially continues on the same subjective day inside the demiplane
after crossing the crack, the DM frames the real chasm as mid-afternoon and Eidrikas later confirms it has been 13 days since Jrain's bird died, compared with the party's expected 9
the resulting four-day discrepancy matches session.yaml ending on 1730-01-29, so this beat spans the transition from the demiplane's 1730-01-27 to the outside world's 1730-01-29
